name: Alto de Amoreira via the Mines of the Shadows – Serra do Xurés
name_original: Alto de Amoreira por las Minas de las Sombras – Serra do Xurés
region_intro: Jogging routes around Lobios offer access to the diverse landscapes of the Parque Natural da Baixa Limia – Serra do Xurés, situated in the province of Ourense, Galicia. The region is characterized by mountainous terrain from the O Xurés and O Gerês ranges, providing varied gradients for runners. Numerous river courses, such as the Río Limia, weave through the landscape, alongside extensive forests and natural pools. This natural setting creates a network of trails suitable for various running preferences. Best jogging routes around Lobios The most popular jogging route is Circular Ruta to Minas dos Carris — Peneda-Gerês National Park, a 13.2 miles (21.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 52 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Alto de Amoreira via the Mines of the Shadows – Serra do Xurés, a difficult 11.9 miles (19.2 km) path. This route offers challenging ascents and views of the Serra do Xurés. Local runners also love the Soajo Granaries loop from Soajo, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail leading through forested areas, often completed in about 46 minutes. Jogging around Lobios is defined by mountainous terrain, river courses, and dense forests. The extensive network of running routes offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging, difficult trails. The routes in Lobios are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 180 runners have used komoot to explore Lobios's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Lobios?
A: Lobios offers a diverse network of over 20 running routes, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find a mix of moderate and challenging trails, with 8 moderate and 13 difficult options to explore.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging trails in Lobios?
A: The jogging trails in Lobios are characterized by mountainous terrain from the O Xurés and O Gerês ranges, offering varied gradients. You'll also encounter numerous river courses like the Río Limia, extensive forests providing shaded paths, and natural pools. This diverse landscape makes for engaging and physically rewarding runs.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Lobios area?
A: Yes, Lobios features several circular running routes. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20147928" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soajo Granaries loop from Soajo&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.1 km trail that takes you through forested areas. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2198022840/circular-ruta-to-minas-dos-carris-peneda-geres-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Ruta to Minas dos Carris — Peneda-Gerês National Park&lt;/a&gt;, a more challenging 21.2 km route with significant elevation gain.

Q: What are some natural attractions or landmarks I might see while running in Lobios?
A: While running in Lobios, you can discover a variety of natural attractions. The region is known for its river courses and natural pools. Specific highlights include the Corga da Fecha Waterfall and its natural pool, which can be reached via popular trails. You might also encounter Roman ruins along routes like the Geira and Arrieiros Way Trail. For those interested in unique natural features, there are also several gorges and river crossings, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4550113" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Swimming spot in Peneda-Gerês National Park&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3543378" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vale de Teixeira&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance running routes in Lobios for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For experienced runners seeking a significant challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2183647162/gr-1-peneda-and-soajo-mountains-crossing" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 1 Peneda and Soajo Mountains Crossing&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 79.5 km route with substantial elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990590095/alto-de-amoreira-via-the-mines-of-the-shadows-serra-do-xures" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Amoreira via the Mines of the Shadows – Serra do Xurés&lt;/a&gt;, an 19.2 km path offering challenging ascents and views.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging experience in Lobios?
A: The running routes in Lobios are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from reviewers. Over 180 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the stunning natural landscapes and the engaging challenge provided by the mountainous trails.

Q: Are there any places to cool down or relax after a run in Lobios?
A: Yes, Lobios is known for its natural hot springs, Caldas de Lobios. While not directly on the trails, these thermal baths offer a relaxing recovery option after strenuous outdoor exercise, aiding circulation and easing muscle pain. Additionally, many routes feature natural pools where you can cool off.

Q: Can I find any shelters or huts along the running trails in Lobios?
A: Yes, there are several shelters and huts in the vicinity of the running trails that can provide a point of interest or a brief respite. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5576859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lomba do Vidoeiro Shepherd Shelter&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5737235" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrucela Mountain Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6750298" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prado Alto Meadow Stone Hut&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Lobios?
A: Lobios offers good jogging conditions for much of the year. The diverse terrain and forests provide shaded paths, which can be pleasant during warmer months. However, given the mountainous nature, spring and autumn often provide the most comfortable temperatures for longer runs, avoiding the peak heat of summer or potential winter snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any running routes that feature waterfalls in the Lobios area?
A: Yes, the region is home to picturesque waterfalls. The Corga da Fecha Waterfall and its natural pool are specific attractions that can be reached via popular trails, offering refreshing sights during a run. You might also encounter waterfalls along the Geira and Arrieiros Way Trail.

Q: What are the general difficulty levels of running routes in Lobios?
A: The running routes around Lobios range from moderate to difficult. There are 8 moderate routes, which are suitable for those looking for a good workout without extreme challenges. For more experienced runners, there are 13 difficult routes that feature significant elevation changes and longer distances, providing a demanding and rewarding experience.
tips: The shadow mines were an extraction point for valuable tungsten, a highly valued strategic material, especially during World War II. The route to the mines is surrounded by fantastic waterfalls.

The summit of Alto da Amoreira is located at an altitude of 1,463 meters, in the south of the province of Orense, in the Serra do Xurés.

Its location on the same border line between Galicia and Portugal allows you to enjoy magnificent views.

At the top, the shape adopted by the granite rocks that come from the glaciers of the Ice Age stands out.
